HttpClient.sidecars.* shipped on @os-eco/burrow with NotFoundError + ValidationError rehydration parity with runs / files; HTTP client tests cover all four methods.

Sidecar lifecycle: process exit → state 'exited' with exit_code captured; DELETE → 'torn-down' (idempotent on already-terminal); burrow DELETE cascades to all sidecars before marking the burrow destroyed; lifecycle transitions emit sidecar_* events on the burrow event stream.
Warren acceptance scenario 20 (warren-8d92) passes on Linux against this burrow build; warren's CLAUDE.md three-places rule is honored when bumping @os-eco/burrow-cli in the warren image.
Burrow SPEC §8.7 + §8.8 are accurate (no drift between contract and implementation); ROADMAP R-08 moves [in-progress] → [shipped] on plan close.
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass; the cross-process integration test in src/server/dispatcher-cross-process.test.ts is updated to exercise the new namespace if needed.

name: R-08 — Inbound port-forwards + sidecar exec endpoint

sections.context: Warren (and any other orchestrator using @os-eco/burrow) needs to thread per-burrow env vars into the sandbox at create time — concretely PLOT_ID and PLOT_ACTOR for the os-eco Plot integration (warren-e26f / warren-a346), but the contract is general (anything an orchestrator wants the in-sandbox agent to see). Today burrow's POST /burrows handler silently drops body.env, so callers think they're injecting env when they aren't. Affected: warren's Plot dispatch path (the in-sandbox plot CLI sees empty PLOT_ID — warren-a346 / scenario 25 has its env-reaches-sandbox + workspace-plot-append + plot.* mirror assertions soft-skipped pending this fix); any future orchestrator-driven per-run secret or coordination var. The fix is plumbing: parseEnvMap and the BurrowUpInput.envOverrides path already exist and are already used by another route; createBurrow just doesn't wire them together. Smallest change with the biggest cross-tool unblock.
sections.reproduction: 1. Start `bun src/cli/main.ts serve --json --port 0 --no-auth` (the mx-a575ee subprocess pattern). 2. POST /burrows with body `{ "projectRoot": "<tmp-repo>", "env": { "FOO": "bar" } }`. 3. Inside the returned burrow, run `printenv FOO`. Observed: FOO is unset. Expected: FOO=bar. Same gap visible without HTTP: callers using `client.burrows.up({ projectRoot, envOverrides: { FOO: 'bar' } })` directly (the in-process path) DO see FOO=bar in the sandbox (asserted by src/server/integration.test.ts:431/460/476) — proving the downstream path works and the gap is isolated to the HTTP handler edge.
sections.root_cause: src/server/handlers.ts createBurrow (lines ~404-437) reads body.{projectRoot, name, branch, baseBranch, originUrl, network, provider, agents, seed} into a BurrowUpInput literal but never reads body.env. parseEnvMap exists at src/server/handlers.ts:854 and is already invoked by another route at line 897 (it returns Record<string,string> | undefined and rejects non-object / non-string-value shapes). BurrowUpInput.envOverrides is plumbed through HttpClient (CLI uses it at src/cli/commands/up.ts:86,154; integration tests assert via envOverrides at lines 431/460/476) all the way to SandboxProfile.setEnv via resolveEnv. The handler is the single missing link: body.env → parseEnvMap → input.envOverrides → client.burrows.up().
sections.approach: Add a parseEnvMap(body.env) call inside createBurrow alongside the existing optional-field reads, assigning to input.envOverrides only when defined (mirroring the conditional-assign pattern used by name/branch/baseBranch/etc. so the field stays undefined-not-empty on absence). Reuse the existing parseEnvMap helper unchanged — same validation contract as line 897. Add an integration test in the same shape as existing envOverrides assertions (mx-a575ee subprocess pattern) so the HTTP edge is covered, not just the in-process Client path. Patch-bump @os-eco/burrow + @os-eco/burrow-cli; this is a bug fix to a documented-but-unimplemented body field. Alternative rejected: mirror the parsing inside a separate /env helper handler — adds surface for no benefit, env is part of burrow creation, not a separate concern.

steps.title: Wire body.env into createBurrow
steps.description: In src/server/handlers.ts createBurrow (~line 404), add `const envOverrides = parseEnvMap(body.env); if (envOverrides !== undefined) input.envOverrides = envOverrides;` alongside the other optional-field reads, BEFORE the `await client.burrows.up(input)` call. Keep insertion order grouped with the other input fields. No changes to parseEnvMap itself.

steps.title: HTTP integration test for body.env round-trip
steps.description: Add a test to src/server/integration.test.ts (or a new file alongside it) in the mx-a575ee subprocess-server pattern: spawn `bun src/cli/main.ts serve --json --port 0 --no-auth`, POST /burrows with `{ projectRoot, env: { FOO: 'bar', BAZ: 'qux' } }`, then exec `printenv FOO BAZ` inside the burrow and assert both values. Also add a negative-case test: POST /burrows with body.env shaped as a non-object (e.g. array or string) returns 4xx with the same error envelope parseEnvMap produces at the other call site (line 897). Tear down the spawned server in afterEach.

steps.title: OpenAPI / wire-doc coverage
steps.description: If src/openapi.yaml (or equivalent schema doc) exists and mentions the POST /burrows body shape, add `env?: Record<string, string>` to the documented schema. If the doc is generated from types, verify BurrowUpInput.envOverrides surfaces in the generated doc and that the request body type accepts the `env` alias used over the wire (no rename — env on wire, envOverrides in TypeScript, mirroring existing). If no such doc exists in this repo, this step is a no-op — note that in the child seed and close it.

steps.title: CHANGELOG + version bump + release prep
steps.description: Add a CHANGELOG entry under a new patch-version section (current is 0.3.x — bump to next patch) describing the fix and naming the warren-side consumer (warren-a346). Run `bun run version:bump patch` to dual-update package.json and src/index.ts VERSION. Run the quality-gate trinity (bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck) with zero warnings. Do NOT publish from this branch — the release workflow handles that on tag push; leave the bump on the branch for the human to tag once merged.

acceptance: POST /burrows with body `{projectRoot, env: {FOO: 'bar'}}` results in `printenv FOO` inside the spawned sandbox returning `bar`.

POST /burrows with body.env shaped as a non-object (array, string, number) returns a 4xx error consistent with parseEnvMap's existing validation at line 897 — same error envelope shape, no half-provisioned burrow.
POST /burrows with no body.env at all is byte-identical to today's behavior — no envOverrides field appears on the BurrowUpInput passed to client.burrows.up.
client.burrows.up({ envOverrides: ... }) in-process callers see no behavior change; existing tests at src/server/integration.test.ts:431/460/476 pass unmodified.
Negative test asserting the validation error shape passes alongside the positive round-trip test.
CHANGELOG.md has a new patch-version entry naming the consumer (warren-a346) and crediting it as the unblocker for warren acceptance scenario 25's PLOT_ID-in-sandbox assertions.
package.json `"version"` and src/index.ts VERSION are bumped consistently to the new patch.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` succeeds with zero warnings (biome --error-on-warnings gate).

sections.context: Quality patrol on 2026-05-27 turned up four small correctness/consistency issues: two CLI --json outputs that aren't pretty-printed (every other --json command emits indent-2), a documented sister-table that drifted out of parity, an internal type-safety smell stashing per-socket state via repeated `as unknown as { __handlers }` casts, and an NDJSON event stream that crashes on the first malformed line because JSON.parse isn't guarded. All findings are scoped to internal restructuring — no new APIs, no dependency changes, no public-signature breaks. The HttpBurrowUpInput-missing-env gap (recorded in mulch mx-d00e99) is filed as a standalone task seed rather than included here because adding a wire field could be read as feature work.
sections.behavior_invariant: Every quality gate stays green: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. CLI exit codes and human-readable output are unchanged. NDJSON event envelope shape is unchanged. Sister-table HTTP status mapping in src/server/errors.ts statusFor() is unchanged. Inbound TCP forward semantics (host port → sandbox netns relay) are unchanged. Only observable diffs allowed: (1) `burrow attach --json` and `burrow upgrade --json` now emit 2-space-indented JSON like every other --json output; (2) HttpEventsClient.stream surfaces a typed error on a malformed NDJSON line instead of letting JSON.parse abort the for-await loop.
sections.approach: One small PR per finding, independent except for the release gate. Each step names the exact file + line range and the shape of the fix. No abstractions extracted, no modules reorganized.

steps.title: Pretty-print attach --json and upgrade --json output
steps.description:
src/cli/main.ts has two --json call sites that emit compact JSON while every other --json command pretty-prints with `JSON.stringify(x, null, 2)`:

- src/cli/main.ts:211 — `burrow attach --json` emits `JSON.stringify({ burrow: result.burrow, wasAlreadyActive: result.wasAlreadyActive })` (no indent).
- src/cli/main.ts:688 — `burrow upgrade --json` emits `JSON.stringify({ command: message, current: VERSION })` (no indent).

Every other --json action (doctor:95, init:124, up:155, fork:186, list:233, show:254, stop:269, destroy:302, prompt:341, send:387, agents list:489, agents show:504, agents validate:518, agents add:537) uses `JSON.stringify(x, null, 2)`. Fix: add `null, 2` to both call sites so the surface is uniform. Add or extend the existing CLI tests for attach + upgrade to assert the formatted output (look for a newline + two-space indent in the body) so the inconsistency can't regress.

steps.title: Align server/errors.ts sister-table comment with CLI exitCodeFor()
steps.description:
src/server/errors.ts:3-4 carries the comment: 'The CLI's exitCodeFor() in src/cli/main.ts is the sister table — keep them aligned.' The two tables have drifted: statusFor() in src/server/errors.ts (line 81) handles 11 BurrowError subclasses (NotFoundError, ValidationError, CredentialError, AgentNotInstalled, AgentRuntimeError, SandboxError, WorkspaceMaterializationError, ToolchainMismatch, SidecarCapExceededError, SecretResolutionError, WorkerDrainingError), while exitCodeFor() in src/cli/main.ts:758 explicitly maps only 5 (ValidationError→3, NotFoundError→2, SandboxError→4, AgentNotInstalled→4, AgentRuntimeError→4) and falls everything else through to 1. Pick ONE direction (don't change observable exit codes without thought):

  (a) Add explicit exitCodeFor() cases for CredentialError, ToolchainMismatch, SidecarCapExceededError, SecretResolutionError, WorkerDrainingError, WorkspaceMaterializationError that return 1 — preserves today's exit codes, restores literal parity, makes intent explicit.
  (b) Update the comment in src/server/errors.ts to clarify the CLI intentionally collapses non-input/non-runtime errors to exit code 1, and that statusFor() is the only place that grows when a new BurrowError subclass lands.

Recommend (a) — keeps the comment honest and gives a single grep target when adding the next error subclass. Add a small unit test in src/cli/main.test.ts or src/server/errors.test.ts that asserts every BurrowError subclass exported from src/core/errors.ts (+ SidecarCapExceededError) has an exitCodeFor() entry, so a future subclass cannot silently regress to default.

steps.title: Drop `as unknown as { __handlers }` socket-state casts in inbound-forward
steps.description: src/provider/local/inbound-forward.ts:214,234,239,243 stash per-socket relay handlers by mutating `(socket as unknown as { __handlers: ... }).__handlers`. Five sites use the same unsafe-cast escape hatch (also called out by noUncheckedIndexedAccess/no-any conventions in CLAUDE.md). Replace with a module-local `WeakMap<Socket, { data?, close? }>`: `open()` sets the entry, `data()`/`close()`/`error()` look it up. No public-API change, no behavior change — just removes the casts and keeps the lint surface clean. Existing tests in src/provider/local/inbound-forward.test.ts already cover relay spawn failure, linux nsenter+nc dispatch, and stop() termination; rerun them as the regression net.

steps.title: Guard JSON.parse in HttpEventsClient NDJSON stream
steps.description:
src/lib/http-client.ts:874 calls `JSON.parse(line) as EventEnvelope` inside the `for await (const line of readNdjsonLines(...))` loop without a try/catch. A single malformed NDJSON line (transport corruption, partial frame, server bug, etc.) throws SyntaxError out of the async generator, killing the iterator and any consumer that was expecting more events.

For comparison, every server-side parser in src/runtime/parsers/* (parsePiEvents, parseJsonlClaude, parseStreamJson) intentionally degrades a malformed line to a synthetic `text` event with a `parseError` payload and keeps going — see the tests at src/runtime/parsers/*.test.ts. The HTTP event tail should be at least as forgiving.

Fix: wrap the JSON.parse + envelopeToRunEvent in try/catch. On parse failure, either (a) skip the line silently (matches NDJSON 'skip blank lines' tolerance already in readNdjsonLines), or (b) throw a typed HttpClientError with code='malformed_event' so callers can decide. Recommend (b) — the rest of the file already promotes wire failures to HttpClientError via rehydrateError(), so callers already handle that error class. Add a test in src/lib/http-client.test.ts that feeds a malformed line into a mocked stream and asserts the iterator either skips it (option a) or throws HttpClientError (option b) instead of bubbling SyntaxError.

sections.context: Code patrol scan on 2026-05-28. Tests, lint, and typecheck are all green. Biome reports only one info-level note about schema URL version skew (2.4.10 vs CLI 2.4.14) which is a config drift not a code defect.
sections.behavior_invariant: All current public behavior is preserved. The in-process Client.burrows.up({ envOverrides }) path, the wire-level POST /burrows body.env parsing via parseEnvMap, and the existing OpenAPI CreateBurrowBody schema remain unchanged. After the fix, callers using HttpBurrowsClient.up({ env: { FOO: 'bar' } }) MUST hit the same code path as the existing raw-fetch tests in src/lib/http-client.test.ts (lines ~247–354), i.e. body.env round-trips through parseEnvMap into BurrowUpInput.envOverrides and lands in SandboxProfile.setEnv. sections.approach: Single small additive change to the HTTP client mirror: add an optional `env?: Record<string, string>` field to HttpBurrowUpInput (src/lib/http-client.ts ~line 98), forward it inside HttpBurrowsClient.up (~line 347) using the same `if (input.env !== undefined) body.env = { ...input.env };` shape that HttpSidecarsClient.create already uses for its env field, and convert the existing raw-fetch tests in src/lib/http-client.test.ts to drive through HttpBurrowsClient.up so the parity is locked in. sections.context: Routine code patrol surfaced four cross-call-site inconsistencies and one shared-logic duplication. None change public APIs or runtime behavior; each lands as a self-contained PR. Findings: (1) `burrow send --json` emits compact JSON while every other one-shot CLI command pretty-prints; (2) the HTTP server's `parseLimit` is missing the `String(n) !== raw` round-trip guard that its sibling integer parsers (`parsePositiveInt`, `parseNonNegativeInt`, CLI `parseLimit`/`parseSince`) all have — so `?limit=10abc` silently parses to 10; (3) `DELETE /burrows/:id?archive=` uses strict `'true'/'false'` while streaming endpoints accept `'true'|'false'|'1'|'0'` per mx-ebd978; (4) `extractWorkspaceSource` (src/lib/destroy.ts) and `extractSource` (src/cli/commands/fork.ts) are byte-identical workspaceSource parsers — drift risk; (5) `parseStreamJson` and `parseJsonlClaude` guard against `null`/non-object via `typeof === 'object'` but not against arrays, so a top-level JSON array is cast `as Record<string, unknown>` and flows through as payload — minor type-safety dishonesty.
sections.behavior_invariant: All existing tests in `bun test` (1139 tests, currently 100% green) keep passing. Public CLI flags, HTTP route paths/methods, OpenAPI golden, and Library API signatures are unchanged. Pretty/JSON renderers preserve their existing keys; only formatting whitespace (indent) is normalized. New parser guards return existing error shapes (`ValidationError` for HTTP, identical thrown error type for parsers) — no new error codes. Stream parsers continue to fall back to a `text` event for unparseable input.
sections.approach: Five independent fixes, each one a single small PR. Step 1 (send --json indent) and steps 2–5 are mutually independent. Each step ships with a targeted test that locks the new behavior. The final step is the standard release gate.

steps.title: Pretty-print `burrow send --json` output
steps.description: src/cli/main.ts:379-388 — the `send` command's --json branch calls `JSON.stringify({ message, deferred, lastAgentId })` without indent args, while every other one-shot --json command (`init`, `up`, `fork`, `attach`, `prompt`, `list`, `doctor`, `upgrade`, `agents list`, `agents validate`, …) uses `JSON.stringify(value, null, 2)`. Sibling fix already shipped for attach/upgrade in burrow-0ce7. Change to `JSON.stringify({...}, null, 2)` and add/update a `send.test.ts` snapshot asserting the indented shape. Streaming NDJSON commands (`chat`, `watch --json`, `ship --json`, `logs`, `events`) stay single-line — they are line-delimited streams, not one-shot results.

steps.title: Fix `parseLimit` to reject trailing garbage in HTTP query params
steps.description: src/server/handlers.ts:162-169 — `parseLimit` validates `Number.isFinite(n) && n >= 1` but is missing the `String(n) !== raw` round-trip check that `parsePositiveInt` (lines 171-178), `parseNonNegativeInt` (lines 180-187), CLI `parseLimit` (src/cli/commands/events.ts:42-50, src/cli/commands/logs.ts:54-61), and CLI `parseSince` (src/cli/commands/logs.ts:45-52) all enforce. Result: `?limit=10abc` silently parses to 10 instead of throwing ValidationError. Add the round-trip guard so the grammar matches its siblings, and extend src/server/handlers.test.ts with a case asserting `?limit=10abc` → 400.

steps.title: Align `?archive=` boolean grammar with streaming endpoints (accept 1/0)
steps.description: src/server/handlers.ts:203-208 — `parseBoolean` (used only by `DELETE /burrows/:id?archive=`) accepts strictly `'true'/'false'`, while `parseStreamBool` (lines 196-200) accepts `'true'|'false'|'1'|'0'` per mx-ebd978 ('streaming endpoints use one shared boolean grammar'). The split is intentional today, but the inconsistency is user-visible: curl users who muscle-memory `?archive=1` get a 400. Two acceptable resolutions: (a) collapse `parseBoolean` into `parseStreamBool` so `?archive=` follows the same grammar (recommended — `archive` is a side-effecting toggle just like `follow`/`once`); or (b) leave the strict variant but inline a code comment + OpenAPI description note explaining why archive is strict. Either way, add a handler test pinning the chosen behavior so it stops drifting. Prefer (a) for fewer special cases. Touch only the parseBoolean call site, the schema description for the `archive` query param in src/server/openapi/spec.ts, and the handler test; regenerate the OpenAPI golden if (a).

steps.title: Consolidate duplicated `workspaceSource` extractor between destroy + fork
steps.description: src/lib/destroy.ts:90-100 (`extractWorkspaceSource`) and src/cli/commands/fork.ts:127-135 (`extractSource`) are byte-identical: both walk `burrow.providerStateJson.workspaceSource` and narrow to `{kind: 'worktree'|'clone', branch: string}` returning `MaterializedWorkspaceSource | null`. Drift risk is real — fork wrote it, destroy duplicated it. Move the helper next to the type at src/provider/local/workspace.ts (where `MaterializedWorkspaceSource` is declared) or into a small src/core/workspace-source.ts module, export it, and replace both call sites. No signature change to any caller, no new module on the public API surface (don't re-export from src/index.ts). Keep the existing tests for both call paths green.

steps.title: Guard NDJSON parsers against top-level JSON arrays
steps.description: src/runtime/parsers/stream-json.ts:30-40 and src/runtime/parsers/jsonl-claude.ts:63-67 both gate on `parsed === null || typeof parsed !== 'object'`, which lets arrays through (arrays satisfy `typeof === 'object'`). They are then cast `as Record<string, unknown>` (stream-json) / `as ClaudeEnvelope` (jsonl-claude) — a dishonest cast that puts an array into the `payload` slot. Add an `Array.isArray(parsed)` branch alongside the null/non-object guard that falls back to the existing `{kind:'text', stream:'stdout', payload:{text: line}}` event (same shape both parsers already emit for the non-object case). Extend src/runtime/parsers/stream-json.test.ts and src/runtime/parsers/jsonl-claude.test.ts with a `'[1,2,3]'` input case verifying the text-fallback envelope. No change to the public parser API.

sections.context: Patrol scan of HEAD on 2026-05-31. Tests/lint/typecheck all green. Recent nightwatch releases (0.3.7, 0.3.8) already covered most of the integer-parser and JSON-output drift. One residual finding: the HTTP/CONNECT proxy's port parser does not match the tight integer grammar adopted across the CLI and server (`String(n) === raw`), so malformed CONNECT targets like `host:80abc` are silently routed to port 80 instead of being rejected.
sections.behavior_invariant: Well-formed inputs continue to behave identically: valid HTTP absolute-URLs and well-formed CONNECT `host:port` targets keep parsing to the same `{hostname, port, ...}` as today, and existing tests in src/proxy/server.test.ts continue to pass unchanged. No public API signatures change.
sections.approach: Tighten the port-validation grammar in src/proxy/server.ts to mirror the canonical CLI parser shape (`String(port) === rawPort`), and add focused regression tests for malformed CONNECT/HTTP targets. Then cut a patch release.

sections.context: Users running self-hosted LLMs that speak the OpenAI API standard cannot point the burrow `pi` runtime at their custom endpoint. Burrow forwards only OPENAI_API_KEY into the sandbox when frontmatter.provider=openai, so OPENAI_BASE_URL set in the container env is silently dropped and non-default endpoints are unreachable. This affects anyone using OpenAI-compatible self-hosted/proxy backends (vLLM, LiteLLM, Ollama OpenAI shim, Azure-style gateways). Reported in https://github.com/jayminwest/burrow/issues/13 by @ConradMearns.
sections.reproduction: 1. Configure a burrow agent with a pi runtime declaring frontmatter.provider=openai. 2. In the sandbox/container environment set both OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL (the latter pointing at a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoint). 3. Launch the agent and inspect the env that reaches the sandboxed `pi` process. Observed: OPENAI_API_KEY is forwarded but OPENAI_BASE_URL is absent, so `pi` falls back to the default api.openai.com endpoint and cannot reach the self-hosted model. Expected: OPENAI_BASE_URL is forwarded into the sandbox alongside OPENAI_API_KEY, mirroring how ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is already forwarded for the anthropic provider.
sections.root_cause: In src/runtime/pi.ts, PI_PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS["openai"] lists only ["OPENAI_API_KEY"]. piEnvPassthrough() unions the per-provider key list onto the anthropic base passthrough (which already includes ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL). Because OPENAI_BASE_URL is not in the openai entry, it is never added to the passthrough set and never reaches the sandboxed pi process. The defect is the missing OPENAI_BASE_URL key in the openai provider entry, not the passthrough plumbing itself, which already handles base-url forwarding correctly for anthropic.
sections.approach: Add "OPENAI_BASE_URL" to PI_PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS["openai"] so it becomes ["OPENAI_API_KEY", "OPENAI_BASE_URL"]. This mirrors the existing ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL entry in the base PI_ENV_PASSTHROUGH triple and reuses the already-correct piEnvPassthrough() union logic — no plumbing changes needed. We implement this fresh ourselves rather than pulling the reporter's fork branch (ConradMearns:feat/openai-base-url-passthrough); the implementation commit and the issue-close comment credit @ConradMearns' proposal. Rejected alternatives: forwarding OPENAI_BASE_URL unconditionally in the base passthrough (would leak it for non-openai providers); special-casing base-url forwarding in piEnvPassthrough (redundant given the existing per-provider key-list mechanism).

sections.context: The resume machinery already exists but nothing wires it, so a run can never resume a prior session (GH #21). piRuntime.buildResumeCommand emits --session <id>; supportsResume is true (claude-code has --resume); extractMetadata captures the pi session_id into Run.metadataJson on exit 0; the resume_of_run_id column exists and RunsRepo.enqueue persists it. But dispatchRun (src/runner/dispatch.ts:131) only ever calls buildSpawnCommand; the createRun handler (src/server/handlers.ts:464) never reads resumeOfRunId from the body; CreateRunBodySchema (src/server/openapi/schemas.ts:369) doesn't document it; and neither RunCreateInput / client.runs.create (src/lib/client.ts:84,293) nor HttpRunCreateInput / the HTTP client create (src/lib/http-client.ts:91,511) forward it. Net effect: an HTTP or library caller cannot resume a session — every run spawns a fresh agent, so multi-turn agents that depend on session continuity can't be built on the public API. The repo owner decided to implement this end-to-end rather than wait for the issue author's promised PR; the issue author's analysis should be credited in the close comment.
sections.approach: Wire the existing resumeOfRunId seam end to end across the three layers, ordered so each layer's input is available before the next consumes it. (1) API + schema + persistence: createRun reads optional resumeOfRunId from the body, threads it through RunCreateInput into the existing RunsRepo.enqueue seam (which already accepts and persists resume_of_run_id), and CreateRunBodySchema documents the field; regenerate the OpenAPI golden. (2) Clients: add resumeOfRunId to RunCreateInput (in-process) and HttpRunCreateInput (HTTP), forwarding it on the wire so HTTP/in-process parity holds. (3) Dispatcher routing: dispatchRun reads run.resumeOfRunId and, when set, validates eligibility (runtime supportsResume; prior run exists, succeeded, same burrowId, same agentId) then routes to runtime.buildResumeCommand(priorRun) instead of buildSpawnCommand — failing fast with a clear, structured errorMessage (same failed-with-message convention dispatchRun already uses for agent/burrow checks) on any ineligibility. This reuses every existing primitive (enqueue column, buildResumeCommand, supportsResume, the failed-with-message pattern at dispatch.ts:84-108) rather than introducing new storage or new runtime APIs. dispatcher.test.ts already asserts the resume argv shape directly via buildResumeCommand with a comment that auto-routing on the spawn path is separate scope — this work closes exactly that gap, so that comment is updated and happy-path + each eligibility-failure mode get dispatcher tests.

alternatives.name: Wait for the issue author's promised PR
alternatives.rejected_because: Repo owner explicitly decided to implement end-to-end ourselves; we credit the author's analysis in the close comment instead of depending on an external PR.

alternatives.name: Resume routing inside the runtime layer rather than dispatchRun
alternatives.rejected_because: dispatchRun is the single place that already chooses buildSpawnCommand and owns the burrow/agent/run context needed for eligibility checks; pushing routing into the runtime would duplicate that context and split the decision across layers.

Dispatcher: dispatchRun validates resume eligibility and routes to buildResumeCommand(priorRun) with fail-fast errors; tests for each failure mode + happy path; run quality gates

The OpenAPI golden (src/server/openapi/__golden__/openapi.json) must be regenerated when CreateRunBodySchema changes, or the golden test fails CI.

Eligibility must fail fast with a clear message (reusing the failed-with-message convention in dispatch.ts:84-108) rather than silently falling back to a fresh spawn, which would mask the caller's resume intent.
FINAL step does a generic patch bump — another plan may also bump patch and ordering isn't guaranteed, so the resulting version number must not be hardcoded anywhere in the plan.
acceptance: POST /burrows/:id/runs accepts an optional resumeOfRunId; createRun reads it and it is persisted on the run row via RunsRepo.enqueue.
CreateRunBodySchema documents resumeOfRunId and the OpenAPI golden (src/server/openapi/__golden__/openapi.json) is regenerated to match.
RunCreateInput (in-process) and HttpRunCreateInput (HTTP) both carry resumeOfRunId; client.runs.create and the HTTP client forward it with HTTP/in-process parity.

When run.resumeOfRunId is set, dispatchRun validates eligibility (runtime supportsResume; prior run exists, succeeded, same burrowId, same agentId) and routes to buildResumeCommand(priorRun); otherwise it uses buildSpawnCommand.

Each eligibility failure mode (runtime lacks resume support, prior run missing, not succeeded, different burrow, different agent) returns a failed RunOutcome with a clear, distinct errorMessage and does NOT fall back to a fresh spawn.
dispatcher.test.ts covers the happy resume path plus each eligibility failure mode, and the stale 'separate scope' comment is updated.
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass.

A patch version bump is applied via bun run version:bump patch (package.json and src/index.ts VERSION in sync), the release flow per .claude/commands/release.md is followed, and GitHub issue #21 is closed with a comment linking the work and crediting the issue author's analysis.
